title: Market Day Mishap
desc: A levitation charm has failed. The market is floating.

steps.text:
The market square was perfectly normal ten minutes ago. Now it's raining upward.

A merchant's levitation charm — used to float heavy crates off the ground for easy transport — has gone haywire. Instead of gently lifting her inventory, it's launched everything skyward. Bolts of cloth spiral like streamers. Ceramic pots tumble in slow motion. A wheel of cheese the size of your head orbits the charm stone at head height, trailing crumbs.

The merchant is standing on a crate that's trying very hard to ascend, stomping it back down with both feet.

"Could someone PLEASE either fix this or catch my cheese?!"

options.label: Try to deactivate the charm stone

steps.text:
The charm stone is a flat disc of carved crystal, pulsing with a frantic blue light. It's spinning on the ground like a top. You try to grab it — it's warm, vibrating, and slippery.

On your third grab, you manage to flip it upside down. The pulse stutters. The floating goods wobble in the air, suspended between up and down.

Then the charm dies entirely. Everything falls at once.

You catch the cheese. A bolt of cloth lands on your head. Three ceramic pots shatter on the cobblestones. The merchant winces at each crack.

"Could have been worse," she says, surveying the damage. "Last time this happened, a crate of live eels went up. Took three days to get them all down."

options.label: Help her sweep up the shards

steps.text:
You start grabbing things out of the air. The cloth is easy — it drifts. The pots are harder — they spin and wobble unpredictably. The cheese is impossible. Every time you get a hand on it, the charm gives a pulse and yanks it just out of reach.

A crowd gathers. Some help. Most watch. A child catches a floating apple and eats it. No one stops her.

After twenty minutes of aerial gymnastics, the charm runs out of energy on its own. The remaining goods settle to the ground in a gentle pile. The merchant counts her inventory.

"Only lost four pots and a bag of spice. That's a good day, honestly." She hands you the cheese. "For your trouble. It's been in the air for a while, but it's still good. Probably."
